questions.question: How much of the year does the prohibition of gas leaf blowers last? What date in the year does it start, and what date does it end?
questions.answer: The prohibition of gas leaf blowers lasts during the clean-up season, which starts on October 26 and ends on December 7 of each year.

questions.question: What are the permitted hours for gas leaf blower operation?
questions.answer: Gas leaf blowers can be operated during the clean-up season on non-holiday weekdays from 8:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m., on Saturdays from 10:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m., and on Sundays from 12:00 p.m. to 4:00 p.m.

questions.question: What are the penalties for violating gas leaf blower regulations?
questions.answer: The penalties for violating gas leaf blower regulations can include a fine not exceeding $250 for the first violation, $500 for the second violation, and $1,000 for the third and any additional violations.

questions.question: Are there any exceptions for school/government/commercial properties?
questions.answer: No, there are no exceptions listed specifically for school, government, or commercial properties regarding the use of internal combustion leaf blowers as stated in this chapter.

questions.question: How are violations reported and enforced?
questions.answer: Violations can be reported through sworn affidavits from two or more unrelated persons who agree on the times and durations of the noise, direct observation by an enforcement officer, or a noise in excess of permissible sound levels measured by a sound level device.

questions.question: Does the ordinance mention the benefits of leaving leaf debris and/or leaf mulching?
Does the ordinance require a report to be presented to the public?
questions.answer: Yes, the Bedford Recreation and Parks Superintendent is required to report annually to the Town Board on the sufficiency of the clean-up season and related details.
